task: Build with small Lego blocks for the nth time with n blocks
raw:
SCENE ANALYSIS - A gray Lego baseplate is on a wooden table. Several small Lego pieces are scattered on the table. The baseplate has printed symbols. STRATEGY - First, grasp a Lego piece from the table. Then, transport it to the baseplate. Finally, place it on the baseplate. Failure modes include dropping the piece during transport or placing it incorrectly. AFFORDANCE REASONING - Step 1 reach the table to grasp a piece; Step 2 grasp the piece at its body; Step 3 transport the piece to the baseplate; Step 4 place the piece on the baseplate.
Goal: any_in(lego_piece, baseplate)
Scene: gray_baseplate, lego_piece, table
Step 1: reach(lego_piece)
  affordance_region: the body of a small lego piece
  contact: gripper_state(open) [progress]
  spatial: distance(gripper, lego_piece, <, 0.03) [completion]
  direction: approach_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: distance(gripper, lego_piece) < 0.03
Step 2: grasp(lego_piece)
  affordance_region: the body of a small lego piece
  contact: holding(lego_piece) [completion]
  direction: grasp_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: holding(lego_piece)
Step 3: transport(lego_piece -> gray_baseplate)
  affordance_region: an empty area on the gray baseplate
  spatial: above(lego_piece, gray_baseplate, 0.05) [completion]
  safety: no_drop(lego_piece)
  done_when: above(lego_piece, gray_baseplate, 0.05)
Step 4: place(lego_piece -> gray_baseplate)
  affordance_region: an empty area on the gray baseplate
  contact: surface_contact(lego_piece, gray_baseplate) [completion]; released(lego_piece) [completion]
  pose: stable(lego_piece) [completion]
  done_when: surface_contact(lego_piece, gray_baseplate) AND released(lego_piece)
<END_OF_PLAN>

steps.affordance_region: the body of a small lego piece
the body of a small lego piece
an empty area on the gray baseplate
an empty area on the gray baseplate
